summary: B11 TOCTOU fixed: commitQuota now atomic via transaction+FOR UPDATE; B11-race GREEN (finalCount=50); W1b B5 preserved; 1 E2-boundary mock refer→telly.

findings.claim: TEST-REVIEW.md W3 B11: checkQuota+commitQuota non-atomic — two concurrent callers both see count=49, both insert, finalCount=51 (TOCTOU)
findings.evidence: commitQuota() rewritten to open a Knex transaction and issue `SELECT COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM ai_command_log WHERE user_id = ? AND created_at >= ? FOR UPDATE` before the INSERT decision. The FOR UPDATE acquires an exclusive range lock on idx_ai_command_log_user_time, serializing concurrent callers. Concurrent caller B blocks at FOR UPDATE until A's transaction commits (count=50), then skips INSERT. DB_PORT=3407 jest quotaTOCTOU: B11-race PASS (finalCount=50, TELLY_VERBOSE=allowedA=true,allowedB=true,finalCount=50); B11-guard PASS. node --check: OK.

findings.claim: Adjacent-regression: E2-boundary mock DB (plain function returning a chain) lacks transaction() — commitQuota now calls db.transaction() and the mock throws TypeError
findings.evidence: Full suite run: 111/112 pass. e2-globalShared.h5 E2-boundary test FAIL: TypeError: db.transaction is not a function at KnexAIUsageRepository.transaction line 104. 7 core A1-A5 e2 tests GREEN. Referred to telly to add transaction(callback) to the mock.

refers.why: e2-globalShared.h5.test.js E2-boundary mock (userBDb function) needs a transaction(callback) method. Minimal: `transaction: async (cb) => cb({ raw: async () => [[{ cnt: 0 }]], insert: fn })`. The 7 core A1-A5 tests are unaffected. Only the E2-boundary quota mechanics test calls commitQuota against the mock DB.
